x-enumeration-literals.name: Valuation Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Is an Assessment that determines the current market value of positions using mark-to-market or mark-to-model methods to ensure accurate financial reporting and risk evaluation.

Comment:
Example: A bank holds 1,000 shares of a stock. The system marks the position to the current market price of $50/share, so the total position value is $50,000.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Compliance Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Is an Assessment that verifies that positions are held in accordance with regulatory, contractual, or internal policy requirements, ensuring compliance and proper authorization.

Comment:
Example: A mutual fund only allows investment-grade bonds. A newly purchased high-yield bond triggers an eligibility check to confirm it complies with fund policy.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Risk Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Is an Assessment that evaluates exposure and risk by monitoring the concentration of positions across asset types, counterparties, or geographies, helping to manage and mitigate portfolio risk.

Comment:
Example: A portfolio has 40% invested in a single technology stock. The system flags this concentration as high risk for review.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Reconciliation Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Is an Assessment that ensures all changes in positionsdue to trades, transfers, or collateral movementsare correctly recorded, reconciled, and aligned with supporting documentation.

Comment:
Example: A derivative trade is executed, but the settlement record shows a mismatch in the notional amount. The reconciliation assessment flags this for correction.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Liquidity Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Is an Assessment that checks whether positions can be converted into cash or collateral as required, supporting funding, margin, and settlement obligations.

Comment:
Example: A bank needs to meet a $5 million margin call. It assesses whether its bond holdings can be liquidated quickly enough to cover the payment.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Discrepancy Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Is an Assessment that identifies errors, mismatches, or missing information in position records and flags them for review or correction to maintain data integrity.

Comment:
Example: A security transfer shows a discrepancy between the trade instruction and custodian record. The exception assessment flags this for manual investigation.
